Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Both XM_021896128.1 and AUR62002256 appear in the paper: CqCsl2 is described as AUR62002256 in Results and XM_021896128.1 in Methods/Accession numbers.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper is on Chenopodium quinoa and uses the abbreviation Cq for this species.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Gene-specific primers were used to amplify CqUGT coding sequences from Bolivian 300-4 grain, and expression/metabolite work was performed in Bolivian 300-4; CqCsl2 sequence was synthesized but studied in the same quinoa accession context.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses were performed in quinoa-derived materials and also transiently in tobacco; as stated, quinoa is one applied species context.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Chenopodium quinoa is the study species for expression and pathway context.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Bolivian 300-4 was the quinoa variety selected for detailed examination.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CqCsl2 is described as a novel CsyGT-like enzyme / Csl-like enzyme involved in triterpenoid glucuronidation.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Results state CqCsl2 is an OA/HE-3-O-glucuronyltransferase.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The study concerns triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis and CqCsl2 is functionally placed in that pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: CqCsl2 catalyzes C-3 glucuronidation of OA/HE, matching a C-3 glucuronidation branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: CqCsl2 acts on aglycones OA and HE before downstream glycosylations, making it an early glycosylation step.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: In vitro assays showed transfer of GlcA from UDP-GlcA to C-3 of OA, giving CE; one-pot assays and summary indicate activity on HE as well.

Primary_Substrate.reason: OA and HE are stated substrates, with UDP-GlcA as donor; substrate specificity and one-pot assays support this.

Primary_Product.reason: CE from OA is explicitly identified, and a 3-O-glucuroniduronic acid-HE intermediate is reported in the one-pot HE reaction.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results show CqCsl2 product CE is substrate for CqUGT73FG1/CqUGT74BB3 and enables synthesis of final saponins such as GRo.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: GRo and related quinoa triterpenoid saponins are produced downstream of the CqCsl2 step.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These downstream compounds are triterpenoid saponins.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Discussion attributes quinoa/triterpenoid saponins generally to bitterness, defense, and pharmacological activities, but not specifically the exact downstream products of CqCsl2.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Spatial metabolomics showed major triterpenoid saponins predominantly localized in the grain pericarp.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: This is a fair summary of the demonstrated biochemical effect: CqCsl2 forms C-3 glucuronidated intermediates required for downstream saponin synthesis.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state CqCsl1 and CqCsl2 had high expression in grains and low expression in seedlings.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same expression pattern is directly reported for quinoa tissues studied.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper states microsomal protein of CqCsl2 was used and could not be purified, but does not explicitly claim native subcellular localization.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The study compares greenhouse-grown quinoa grains and 15-day seedlings and also tests transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are reported for CqCsl2: expression-guided selection, in vitro LC-MS assay, donor specificity, kinetics, one-pot synthesis, and transient expression.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports it as a key entry glycosylation step; it does not establish rate-limiting status, matching the field wording.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The pEAQ-HT vector is explicitly stated, but the paper does not specify the promoter in this extracted field wording.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA1169548 and SAMN44078480-85 are explicitly given; metabolomics is reported without a deposition accession.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by Results and Summary.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly states these genes provide candidate target genes for breeding lower-saponin quinoa and tools for metabolic engineering of medicinal triterpenes.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion supports the tradeoff concept: lowering saponins can aid edibility, while saponins also function in defense and have pharmacological value.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states 94.4% identity to CqCsl1 and strict UDP-GlcA donor specificity.
